title: Indiscretions of Archie
description: Indiscretions of Archie — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.description: The year 1921 — publication date of Indiscretions of Archie and the novel's approximate present, set in Prohibition-era New York with the cultural atmosphere of the early Jazz Age.

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Indiscretions of Archie" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Alice Wigmore
memes.description: Fiancée of the illustrator J. B. Wheeler and painter of "The Wigmore Venus," an oil painting that becomes the central MacGuffin of the novel's final chapters.

memes.label: Anglo-American Relations
memes.description: The cultural contrast and mutual perception between English and American characters in Indiscretions of Archie.

memes.label: Appearance and First Impressions
memes.description: How characters in the novel are judged by their appearance — clothing, manner, and physical presence — and how these first impressions are both essential to the social comedy and consistently misleading.

memes.label: Archie's English Slang
memes.description: The distinctive English slang expressions used by Archie Moffam that mark his foreignness in American society — "dash it," "good egg," "old bean," "jolly old," and other Edwardian colloquialisms that define his comic voice.

memes.label: Archie Moffam
memes.description: The protagonist of Indiscretions of Archie — a penniless but irrepressibly cheerful young Englishman who marries Lucille Brewster and must navigate the hostile terrain of his American father-in-law's world.

memes.label: Archie's Lemon-Coloured Bathing Suit
memes.description: A vivid lemon-coloured two-piece bathing suit worn by Archie Moffam at the Hotel Cosmopolis, described by Archie himself as "the good old pivot" of his early misadventures.

memes.label: Arrival at the Hotel Cosmopolis
memes.description: Archie Moffam's arrival at the Hotel Cosmopolis, which sets the entire plot in motion — his meeting with Brewster, his criticism of the hotel, and the beginning of the central conflict.

memes.label: Art and Collecting
memes.description: The role of art collecting and the art market as a source of comic episodes in the novel — reflecting tensions between aesthetic value, market value, and cultural aspiration in 1920s New York.

memes.label: Bill Brewster
memes.description: Lucille Brewster's brother and Archie Moffam's brother-in-law, whose romance with chorus girl Mabel Winchester drives Chapter XVII.

memes.label: blake
memes.description: A minor character referenced in the novel.

memes.label: Boy Scouts of America
memes.description: The American scouting organization referenced by Archie Moffam as a benchmark of good-deed-doing — a comic measuring stick for his own moments of altruism.

memes.label: Bradstreet
memes.description: Bradstreet's credit reference book — invoked sarcastically by Daniel Brewster to suggest Archie married Lucille for money after checking her father's financial standing.

memes.label: British Army
memes.description: The British military force in which Archie Moffam served as a second lieutenant during the First World War — a defining biographical detail that shapes his character and social standing.

memes.label: Broadway
memes.description: The iconic New York thoroughfare that serves as the glittering backdrop for Archie Moffam's American adventures, from chance encounters to theatrical ambitions.

memes.label: Business and Enterprise
memes.description: The comic misadventures of Archie and other characters in the world of American business, where English amateurism collides with commercial enterprise.

memes.label: China Warrior Figurine
memes.description: A small Ming Dynasty china figurine of a warrior with a spear, part of a prized pair in Daniel Brewster's art collection, whose missing counterpart drives a subplot involving Mawson the art agent.

memes.label: Cigar-Stand Girl
memes.description: An employee at the Hotel Cosmopolis cigar counter who serves as Archie's key informant on the romantic status of Gus "Looney" Biddle and the Girl Friend.

memes.label: Clubs and Social Institutions
memes.description: The role of gentlemen's clubs and social institutions as markers of status and sites of male sociability in the novel's New York setting.

memes.label: Concept Index
memes.description: A dynamic index of all concept pages in the wiki, sorted alphabetically. Covers themes, social practices, cultural references, and abstract ideas explored in the novel.

memes.label: Conversation and Dialogue
memes.description: The art of conversation as the primary vehicle of Wodehouse's comic method — where misunderstandings, revelations, and reconciliations are enacted through brilliantly orchestrated dialogue.

memes.label: Cosmopolitan Magazine
memes.description: The American general-interest magazine that originally serialized Indiscretions of Archie in 1920 before its publication as a book.

memes.label: Daniel Brewster
memes.description: The self-made millionaire owner of the Hotel Cosmopolis and father of Lucille — Archie's reluctant father-in-law whose pride in his hotel and suspicion of his English son-in-law drive the novel's central conflict.

memes.label: Dash It
memes.description: Archie Moffam's characteristic British exclamation of frustration and mild annoyance, used throughout the novel.

memes.label: Dining and Social Ritual
memes.description: The role of meals and dining rituals as social theater in the Hotel Cosmopolis — where breakfasts, lunches, and dinners become stages for negotiation, confrontation, and comic mishap.

memes.label: Elmer M. Moon
memes.description: A clothing merchant whose name appears on a shop panel, described as "a hustler" who designs Archie's hotel wardrobe. Archie finds him elusive and his ethics questionable.

memes.label: Elopement of Archie and Lucille
memes.description: The off-stage elopement of Archie Moffam and Lucille Brewster — the event that precedes the novel's opening and creates its central dramatic situation.

memes.label: England
memes.description: Archie's country of origin — the implied cultural and geographical backdrop of English society from which he has departed and against which his American adventures are measured.

memes.label: Event Index
memes.description: A dynamic index of all event pages — subplots, backstories, climaxes, and historical references that structure the novel's narrative.

memes.label: Failure and Resilience
memes.description: The recurring pattern of failure and recovery that structures the novel's episodic comedy — Archie's schemes collapse, but his spirit never does.

memes.label: Fatherhood and Authority
memes.description: The role of patriarchal authority in the novel, centered on Daniel Brewster's relationship with his son-in-law Archie and his daughter Lucille — a generational comedy of power, pride, and reluctant acceptance.

memes.label: Fifth Avenue
memes.description: New York's premier thoroughfare, lined with the fashionable clubs and luxury establishments that define the novel's social geography.

memes.label: Friendship
memes.description: The role of friendship and male camaraderie as a social force that drives episodes and resolves conflicts in the novel.

memes.label: General Mannister
memes.description: A General who appears briefly in Chapter VII to steer Archie Moffam through a crush at a hotel gathering.

memes.label: Gentlemanly Conduct
memes.description: The ideal of the English gentleman as embodied by Archie Moffam — a code of amiability, fair play, and imperturbable good humor that proves unexpectedly effective in the American context.

memes.label: George Benham
memes.description: A playwright and theatre director at the Hermitage resort, friend of Archie Moffam, whose production starring Miss Silverton is thrown into chaos by the bulldog Percy.

memes.label: George H. Doran Company
memes.description: New York publishing house that issued the first American edition of Indiscretions of Archie in 1921.

memes.label: Good Egg
memes.description: Archie Moffam's recurring catchphrase expressing approval, one of his distinctive period slang expressions that encapsulates his comic optimism.

memes.label: "Greenwich Village"
memes.description: "A bohemian neighborhood in lower Manhattan, associated in the novel with artiness and affectation among the young women of the Middle West."

memes.label: gus
memes.description: A minor character referenced in the novel.

memes.label: "Health Bread"
memes.description: "A whole-grain bread associated with Mrs. Cora Bates McCall's 'Rational Eating' movement, serving as a recurring comic motif symbolising the clash between dietary reform and human appetite."

memes.label: Hope and Optimism
memes.description: Archie Moffam's defining psychological trait — an unshakeable optimism that transforms setbacks into opportunities and failures into anecdotes.

memes.label: Hotel Cosmopolis
memes.description: The luxury New York hotel owned by Daniel Brewster that serves as the novel's primary setting and social hub.

memes.label: Hotel Cosmopolis
memes.description: The luxury New York hotel owned by Daniel Brewster — the novel's primary setting, a monument to its proprietor's pride and the stage for Archie Moffam's most memorable indiscretions.

memes.label: Indiscretions of Archie (the Novel)
memes.description: Indiscretions of Archie — the 1921 novel by P. G. Wodehouse, published by George H. Doran Company in New York, after initial serialization in Cosmopolitan magazine (1920).

memes.label: International Magazine Company
memes.description: Parent company of Cosmopolitan Magazine and holder of the 1920 copyright for the novel's original serialization.

memes.label: J. B. Wheeler
memes.description: An artist and painter who appears in the novel's art-related episodes — a figure whose work and personality intersect with the commercial and aesthetic themes of the story.

memes.label: James Gossett
memes.description: A motion-picture figure referenced in the novel, connecting the story to the emerging film industry of 1920s America.

memes.label: James J. Gossett
memes.description: An American motion-picture magnate who becomes an unwitting victim in the Sausage Chappie's restaurant rampage, having a fruit salad decanted over his bald head.

memes.label: Leisure and Sports
memes.description: The role of leisure pursuits — particularly golf and baseball — as social arenas where character is revealed and business is transacted in unexpected ways.

memes.label: Letters and Correspondence
memes.description: The role of letters, telegrams, and written correspondence as plot devices that deliver crucial information, create misunderstandings, and drive narrative turns.

memes.label: biddle
memes.description: A minor character referenced in the novel.

memes.label: Lord Seacliff
memes.description: Archie Moffam's old school friend, a young English lord with a weakness for drink, brought to America by his uncle General Mannister to escape temptation during Prohibition.

memes.label: Love and Marriage
memes.description: The central romantic plotline of Archie and Lucille's impulsive marriage and its consequences, and the novel's comic treatment of matrimony.

memes.label: Lucille Brewster
memes.description: The daughter of Daniel Brewster and wife of Archie Moffam — the novel's most capable character, whose practical wisdom and emotional intelligence manage both her father's temper and her husband's chaos.

memes.label: Lucille's Bracelet
memes.description: A piece of Lucille Brewster's jewelry that becomes a financial resource in times of need — pawned by Archie and Lucille to raise funds, embodying the couple's precarious financial situation and mutual devotion.

memes.label: Mawson
memes.description: Daniel Brewster's art agent who scouts for collectibles in New York, most notably discovering a rare china warrior figurine and being tasked with finding its missing pair.

memes.label: Maxie Blumenthal
memes.description: A jovial theatrical businessman whom Archie and Daniel Brewster entertain over lunch, described as a "bird" with a fondness for working-man themes and theatrical investments.

memes.label: Miami
memes.description: A Florida resort destination referenced in the novel — part of the geography of American leisure that extends beyond New York.

memes.label: "The Middle West"
memes.description: "The central region of the United States, portrayed in the novel as a source of earnest provincial types seeking sophistication in New York."

memes.label: "Ming Dynasty"
memes.description: "The Chinese imperial dynasty (1368–1644) referenced in connection with a valuable china figurine that drives a key plotline in the novel."

memes.label: Miss Vera Silverton
memes.description: Miss Vera Silverton is a "spectacular" stage actress and singer — a vivid supporting character with theatrical presence, "compelling eyes," and a talent for creating comic misunderstandings that entangle Archie Moffam and playwright George Benham.

memes.label: Miss Silverton's Pistol
memes.description: A small automatic pistol brandished by actress Miss Silverton during a confrontation at the Hermitage resort.

memes.label: Mr. Connolly
memes.description: character who appears in the novel's social and business episodes

memes.label: Mr. McCall
memes.description: A meek, bespectacled man henpecked by his domineering wife Cora Bates McCall, who initiates a lawsuit against Daniel Brewster over Westchester property.

memes.label: Tuyl
memes.description: Mr. van Tuyl is Reggie van Tuyl's father — a wealthy New York gentleman whom Archie consults on matters of taste and who appears peripherally in the novel's social episodes.

memes.label: Mrs. Cora Bates McCall
memes.description: Rational Eating lecturer and mother of Washy McCall, whose dietary tyranny over her family is undone by her son's pie-eating triumph.

memes.label: New York Chronicle
memes.description: A New York morning newspaper read by Mrs. McCall at breakfast, in whose pages she anxiously searches for coverage of her lecture at the Butterfly Club.

memes.label: New York City
memes.description: The American metropolis that serves as the novel's setting — a city of hotels, clubs, and commercial energy into which Archie Moffam arrives as an English outsider in the early 1920s.

memes.label: New York Giants
memes.description: The New York Giants professional baseball team — a recurring reference in the novel whose fortunes on the field intersect with the betting activities of Archie and his circle.

memes.label: "Ninth Avenue"
memes.description: "A Manhattan thoroughfare where Archie Moffam walks despondently after the Looney Biddle incident."

memes.label: Officer Cassidy
memes.description: A New York police officer who arrests Archie in Chapter V, engages in comic banter with his partner Officer Donahue, and is described as having a "granite" expression and speaking with an Irish accent.

memes.label: Officer Donahue
memes.description: A New York police officer, partner of Officer Cassidy, who participates in Archie's arrest and engages in comic dialogue marked by Irish nationalism and gloomy predictions about the Shannon river.

memes.label: "Old Bean"
memes.description: Archie Moffam's signature term of endearment — "old bean" — a recurring catchphrase that marks both his English identity and the novel's climax when Mr. Brewster adopts it.

memes.label: Organization Index
memes.description: A dynamic index of all organization pages — baseball teams, publishers, military units, businesses, and social clubs referenced in the novel.

memes.label: P. G. Wodehouse
memes.description: Sir Pelham Grenville Wodehouse (1881–1975), the English author and humorist who wrote Indiscretions of Archie (1921) — one of the most celebrated comic writers in the English language, creator of Jeeves, Blandings Castle, and the indomitably cheerful Archie Moffam.

memes.label: Parker
memes.description: A member of the Hotel Cosmopolis staff who appears in the novel's service-world episodes.

memes.label: Pauline Preston
memes.description: A woman referenced in Chapter XXIV whose "wallop is vouched for," appearing in a police-court context with her sister. A minor referenced character.

memes.label: Pen-and-Ink Club
memes.description: A New York social club and coffee-house frequented by artists, writers, and press agents, where Archie Moffam meets J. B. Wheeler and becomes part of the city's Bohemian set.

memes.label: "Percy the Bulldog"
memes.description: "A formidable bulldog belonging to a woman who brings him to theatrical rehearsals, prominently featured when Archie encounters him in his hotel room and later when the dog falls ill."

memes.label: Person Index
memes.description: A dynamic index of all person pages in the wiki, sorted alphabetically. Rendered automatically from the pages database — no manual maintenance required.

memes.label: Pip-Pip, Cheerio, Toodle-oo
memes.description: Archie Moffam's cluster of period farewell expressions — "pip-pip," "cheerio," "toodle-oo," and "good-bye-ee" — used in rapid succession as comic verbal punctuation.

memes.label: Pittsburg Pirates
memes.description: The Pittsburg Pirates professional baseball team — rivals of the New York Giants and the opposing team in the baseball game on which Archie wagers his fortune.

memes.label: Place Index
memes.description: A dynamic index of all place pages in the wiki, sorted alphabetically. Rendered automatically from the pages database — new place pages appear here automatically.

memes.label: Plucky Actress Captures Burglar
memes.description: Imagined newspaper headline by actress Miss Silverton during the hotel room mix-up with Archie, revealing her hunger for publicity.

memes.label: Pongo
memes.description: A small antique china figurine of a warrior in armour with a spear, dating to the Ming Dynasty, owned by Daniel Brewster and central to a major subplot involving Archie Moffam and Professor Binstead.

memes.label: Poulet en Casserole
memes.description: A classic French chicken casserole dish, ordered by Archie Moffam from room service at the Hotel Cosmopolis — its aromatic arrival captivates the food-deprived Washington McCall.

memes.label: Professor Binstead
memes.description: An old friend of Daniel Brewster and an enthusiastic collector of curios — a genial academic figure who serves as ally and confidant to both Brewster and Archie.

memes.label: Project Gutenberg
memes.description: The digital library project that produced the public domain electronic text of Indiscretions of Archie, used as the source material for this wiki.

memes.label: Quote Index
memes.description: A dynamic index of all quote pages — memorable lines, catchphrases, dialogue excerpts, and notable narrative passages from the novel.

memes.label: "Rallying Round Percy"
memes.description: "The chapter XIII episode in which Archie returns to his hotel room to find it occupied by a stranger's bulldog, leading to a series of comic confrontations and an eventual reconciliation with the dog's owner."

memes.label: "Rational Eating"
memes.description: "A dietary philosophy lectured upon by Cora Bates McCall, emphasizing scientific food selection over indulgence."

memes.label: Reggie van Tuyl
memes.description: A friend of Archie Moffam who introduces him to the world of baseball wagering and New York sporting life.

memes.label: Roscoe Sherriff
memes.description: A business associate and minor character who features in Archie's commercial misadventures.

memes.label: Salvatore
memes.description: A dark, sinister-looking Italian immigrant waiter at the Cosmopolis Hotel grill-room who is dismissed by Daniel Brewster, then exacts revenge by refusing to sell his mother's newspaper shop — which sits on the site of Brewster's planned new hotel.

memes.label: The Sausage Chappie
memes.description: A war veteran with amnesia who saved Archie Moffam's life at St. Mihiel by sharing a sausage during the First World War; later works as a waiter at the Cosmopolis Grill, throws a pie at movie magnate James Gossett, and is reunited with his wife after recovering his memory.

memes.label: Seventh Avenue
memes.description: The New York avenue where Salvatore's mother owns a newspaper and tobacco shop — a small commercial property that becomes the unexpected linchpin of Daniel Brewster's expansion plans.

memes.label: "Sixth Avenue"
memes.description: "A major north-south thoroughfare in Manhattan, appearing in the novel as a locus of commercial activity including shops and restaurants."

memes.label: Spectatia Huskisson
memes.description: A loud-voiced aspiring singer from Snake Bite, Michigan, who becomes Bill Brewster's love interest and performs at the Hotel Cosmopolis — the centerpiece of Archie's scheme to help both his composer friend and his brother-in-law.

memes.label: Springfield, Ohio
memes.description: The Ohio hometown of the Sausage Chappie — the place whose name emerges through the fog of his amnesia as the first recovered fragment of his pre-war identity.

memes.label: Battle of St. Mihiel
memes.description: A World War I battle near St. Mihiel, France, where the Sausage Chappie saved Archie Moffam's life by sharing a piece of sausage — an act of camaraderie that becomes the emotional centre of a later reunion scene.

memes.label: "Stage Star and Midnight Marauder"
memes.description: Miss Silverton's wistful imagined newspaper headlines — "Stage Star and Midnight Marauder" and "Footlight Favourite Foils Felon" — revealing her theatrical ambitions and flair for self-dramatization.

memes.label: "Summer Storms"
memes.description: "A chapter-length subplot in which Archie attempts to mediate a lovers' quarrel between the Girl Friend and pitcher Gus 'Looney' Biddle before a crucial baseball game."

memes.label: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
memes.description: Arthur Conan Doyle's classic detective collection, read by Lord Seacliff (Squiffy) in Chapter VIII — his choice of "The Speckled Band" for light bedtime reading.

memes.label: The Art Purchase Negotiation
memes.description: A comic episode in which Archie becomes embroiled in art-market negotiations — a characteristic Wodehouse set-piece where English amateurism meets American commerce.

memes.label: The Baseball Bet
memes.description: Archie's impulsive $1000 bet with Reggie van Tuyl on a New York Giants–Pittsburgh Pirates game — a wager that hinges entirely on the romantic status of pitcher Gus "Looney" Biddle and drives the chase sequence across chapters 17–20.

memes.label: The Bulldog
memes.description: A "great whacking brute of a bulldog" (014-111) that appears in multiple episodes at the Hotel Cosmopolis, serving as a recurring comic prop and silent witness to Archie Moffam's adventures.

memes.label: The Girl Friend
memes.description: The unnamed girlfriend of New York Giants pitcher Gus "Looney" Biddle, whose romantic quarrels with Gus directly cause his pitching collapses — making her an unwitting driver of the novel's baseball subplot.

memes.label: The Grill-Room
memes.description: The restaurant within the Hotel Cosmopolis where Archie Moffam dines, Salvatore works, and the Sausage Chappie achieves his pie-throwing apotheosis — the setting for some of the novel's most memorable comic scenes.

memes.label: The Hermitage
memes.description: A summer resort hotel where Miss Silverton's theatrical show is in rehearsal and Lucille Brewster stays — the setting for the Percy-the-bulldog incident and its chaotic aftermath.

memes.label: The Hotel Criticism Incident
memes.description: The pivotal comic confrontation in which Archie, unaware of Brewster's identity, calls the Hotel Cosmopolis "a bally rotten hotel" — triggering the novel's central conflict between English insouciance and American pride.

memes.label: The Iliad
memes.description: Homer's ancient Greek epic poem, invoked by the narrator of Chapter XXX in a self-deprecating verse passage comparing his own descriptive powers unfavorably to Homer's.

memes.label: The Jeweller
memes.description: A stout, comfortable jeweller whose shop is near the Hotel Cosmopolis — he conducts business with Archie Moffam with approving approval in Chapter XVII.

memes.label: The Marriage Revelation
memes.description: The dramatic scene in which Daniel Brewster learns — through a telegram and letter — that his daughter Lucille has married Archie Moffam without his knowledge, transforming the hotel criticism incident from a minor social offense into a family catastrophe.

memes.label: The Personality That Wins
memes.description: A self-help treatise on personal magnetism — bought by Archie for two dollars to coach his brother-in-law Bill Brewster in the art of winning personalities, with comically stiff results.

memes.label: "The Sad Case of Looney Biddle"
memes.description: "The episode in which Archie Moffam accidentally injures the star pitcher Looney Biddle's left hand, costing him his bet money and Lucille's birthday present."

memes.label: The Telegram
memes.description: The fateful telegram that informs Daniel Brewster of his daughter Lucille's marriage to Archie Moffam — the plot device that transforms a hotel quarrel into a family conflict.

memes.label: The Wheeler Painting
memes.description: A painting by the artist J. B. Wheeler that becomes a central object of desire, negotiation, and comic confusion in the novel's art-market subplot.

memes.label: The Wigmore Venus
memes.description: An oil painting by Alice Wigmore depicting a comfortably plump Venus with a dove — universally disliked except by Archie and its painter — that becomes the central MacGuffin driving the comic climax of the novel.

memes.label: The Wigmore Venus Affair
memes.description: A multi-chapter subplot in which Archie steals the Wigmore Venus painting as an anniversary gift, Lucille regifts it to her father, and Mr. Brewster's hatred of the painting culminates in Archie destroying it — an act that finally earns Brewster's respect.

memes.label: Vera Silverton
memes.description: This page has been merged into miss-silverton — the canonical page for Miss Vera Silverton.

memes.label: Washington "Washy" McCall
memes.description: Teenage son of Mr. and Mrs. McCall who becomes an unlikely pie-eating champion.

memes.label: Wealth and Money
memes.description: The role of money and financial status as a driving force in the novel's plot and character relationships.

memes.label: "What Ho"
memes.description: Archie Moffam's characteristic exclamation — "What ho!" — an Edwardian greeting that marks his cheerful English gentility in the American setting.

memes.label: Willie
memes.description: A "dog-faced" assistant in an art shop who serves Archie Moffam with a mix of suspicion and commercial acumen, appearing primarily in Chapter XI.

memes.label: Wilson Hymack
memes.description: An aspiring composer and Archie's friend from the war (Armentières), who writes the hit song "It's a Long Way Back to Mother's Knee" — which, when performed at the Hotel Cosmopolis, accidentally resolves a labour strike and becomes a national sensation.

memes.label: World War I
memes.description: World War I (1914–1918) — the global conflict that forms the recent backstory of the novel, in which Archie Moffam served as a second lieutenant and which shadows the characters' lives.
